page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384 is a memory-optimized server instance within the c4d family. It features 384 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 3024.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides approximately 7.88 GB of RAM per core, delivering a high-density memory profile designed for large-scale in-memory computing.

This server configuration does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached storage options. Additionally, it does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local NVMe storage and hardware accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, positioning this instance purely for memory-bound and CPU-heavy workloads that do not require local disk speed or GPU acceleration.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c4d-highmem-384 offers high resource density, consolidating massive memory and compute power into a single virtual machine to reduce the overhead of managing multi-node clusters. This makes it highly suitable for large-scale databases, in-memory analytics, enterprise resource planning systems, and intensive ETL data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-384 is a memory-optimized server instance from the c4d family, running on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 384 dedicated vCPUs and 3024.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 7.88 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. The absence of local storage and hardware acceleration represents a design tradeoff, focusing resources entirely on massive memory capacity and compute density. Qualitatively, the server provides high resource consolidation, reducing cluster management overhead for large workloads. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications, including large-scale databases, in-memory data processing, enterprise resource planning systems, and complex ETL pipelines.

tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual server featuring three terabytes of RAM and three hundred eighty-four dedicated x86_64 cores.
bullet_points: 3024.0 GB of system memory providing 7.88 GB per core
No local storage or integrated GPUs included
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and ETL pipelines
High resource density to minimize multi-node cluster overhead
